summary: This is an extremely brief post where Joel Spolsky acknowledges the passage of New York State's Marriage Equality Act in June 2011. The post contains no substantive text beyond the title, serving as a simple marker of the event. It represents a rare instance of Joel using his software-focused blog to note a political and social milestone. The lack of commentary suggests he viewed the headline as speaking for itself.
key_points: Joel marked the passage of NY's Marriage Equality Act on his software blog with no additional commentary
The post signals Joel's support for marriage equality through the choice to highlight it on his platform
It represents a departure from Joel's typical software development and business topics
key_insight: Sometimes a headline is the entire statement, and choosing to amplify a moment on your platform is itself a form of commentary.
